selectedOption: Adopt a versioned, persistent, locally editable Demo Sandbox derived from an immutable Demo template, with explicit reset behavior and no Firebase, myTrips, or Offline Cache writes. Clone uses the current validated Sandbox snapshot. Add a replayable high-level Feature Introduction in the Lobby and Settings while preserving the existing trip-contextual Feature Tour as a separate action.
rationale: Supersede the original six-Assignment Gate 2 plan with this revised nine-Assignment plan; preserve the original Session as immutable audit history and keep its Assignments non-executable.

The immutable source boundary requires createTokyoDemoTrip to return a fresh source object every time. The application never persists or mutates that source object, prevents nested-reference mutation leakage, and rebuilds Reset state from the latest source template.

Store the local Sandbox under the versioned key travel-app-demo-sandbox-v1 with at least schemaVersion, templateVersion, sandboxId, trip, createdAt, and updatedAt. Use sandboxId local-demo-sandbox only as local identity; never treat it as a real Firebase room ID or add it to myTrips.

Run localStorage write/read/remove availability preflight, parse JSON defensively, validate schema and template versions, and return a defensive copy on every read. Surface explicit write errors. When storage is unavailable, allow an in-memory Sandbox with a clear warning that refresh will lose changes; never connect Firebase because local persistence failed.
Support itinerary place add, edit, delete, reorder, arrival-time changes, and note changes plus Checklist add, edit, toggle, delete, and owner-or-unassigned assignee selection.

Do not add expense, settlement, ticket, attachment, Storage upload, or Firebase collaboration editing in this Amendment. Any retained example content remains clearly preview-only and produces no cloud side effect.

Reset uses a confirmation dialog, clears only the local Sandbox key, and rebuilds from the latest templateVersion. It does not affect myTrips, Offline Cache, real Firebase trips, first-run state, or the immutable source template.

Clone consumes the current Sandbox snapshot, validates it through the Sandbox validator, and then applies the approved Clone allowlist converter. It must never deep-copy Sandbox state, must exclude every field prohibited by the original Decision, and must not automatically reset the Sandbox after successful Clone.

Add a visible Lobby 功能介紹 button and a Settings 功能介紹 item. FirstRunWelcomeDialog supports replay mode without reading or writing first-run completion, eligibility, seen, or dismissal state; replay still allows opening Demo or creating a trip.

Preserve Settings 功能導覽 as the distinct TripDetail contextual spotlight tour. 功能介紹 describes high-level App capabilities; 功能導覽 operates only after a trip is open. They require separate copy, test IDs, accessible names, and handlers.

Retain every existing Clone safety requirement: owner-only output, Clone CTA only inside Demo, disabled-by-default feature flag, Emulator-only technical development, no production Firebase, no Firebase Rules change, no dependency, no migration, and no deployment.
acceptedArguments: Accept the human product owner's confirmed requirement for editable itinerary and Checklist behavior, refresh persistence, explicit Reset, local-only isolation, and separate Feature Introduction replay entries.

Accept the human reviewer's requirement that the source template remain immutable, persisted data be versioned and untrusted, corruption rebuild safely, Reset remain bounded, and Clone revalidate the current snapshot.
Accept strict separation between high-level 功能介紹 replay and existing trip-contextual 功能導覽, including independent state, labels, accessibility contracts, and tests.
Accept retention of the original owner-only, allowlist, feature-flag, Emulator-only, no-Rules, no-production, no-dependency, no-migration, and no-deploy safeguards.
Accept the original Session and six Assignments as immutable audit history while declaring that plan superseded and non-executable.
rejectedAlternatives: Keeping Demo Preview read-only, because the human explicitly confirmed an editable local Sandbox.
Persisting Demo edits to Firebase, myTrips, Offline Cache, or Storage, because Sandbox edits are device-local demonstration data.
Mutating or persisting the createTokyoDemoTrip source object, because Reset and future sessions require a clean immutable template.
Deep-copying an unvalidated Sandbox directly into a real trip, because untrusted local state must pass Sandbox validation and the Clone allowlist converter.
Editing expenses, settlements, tickets, attachments, or collaboration in this Amendment, because those domains are outside the confirmed scope and increase cloud-side-effect risk.
Combining 功能介紹 and 功能導覽 or reusing first-run completion side effects in replay mode.
Executing the original six Assignments or any revised Assignment before Revised Gate 2 approval.
risks: Nested reference reuse could leak Sandbox mutations into the source template.
Corrupted, stale, incompatible, or manually modified localStorage data could produce invalid Demo or Clone state.
localStorage failure could silently lose edits or tempt a fallback to Firebase.
Reset could be confused with Clone or affect unrelated local or cloud data.
Editable Demo could be mistaken for a real collaborative trip.
Clone could use an invalid or stale snapshot or carry preview-only domains into a real trip.
Feature Introduction replay could alter first-run state or be conflated with Feature Tour.
Expanded orchestration could accidentally write Offline Cache, myTrips, Storage, or production Firebase.
The superseded original Assignments could be executed by mistake.
mitigations: Generate fresh source objects and defensive copies at template, store-read, editor, Reset, and Clone boundaries; add mutation-leakage regression tests.
Version and validate every Sandbox record and snapshot as untrusted input; safely rebuild from the template on corruption or incompatibility without cloud writes.
Use localStorage availability preflight, explicit persistence errors, and a clearly labelled memory-only fallback that never invokes Firebase.
Give Reset a separate confirmation and handler that removes only travel-app-demo-sandbox-v1 and rebuilds local state.
Show a persistent local-only Sandbox status and exclude local-demo-sandbox from room and myTrips semantics.
Validate the current snapshot before allowlist conversion; preserve all original forbidden-data tests and do not reset after Clone.
Implement an explicit replay mode with no onboarding storage side effects and separate 功能介紹/功能導覽 copy, test IDs, accessible names, and tests.
Keep feature flag disabled by default, technical writes Emulator-only, and add negative tests for Offline Cache, myTrips, Storage, production Firebase, Rules, dependencies, and migrations.
Record supersession in Session facts, ADRs, risks, tests, and Revised Gate 2; only the nine revised execution-disabled Assignments may be considered for later approval.
requiredTests: Store tests for schemaVersion, templateVersion, local-demo-sandbox identity, write/read/remove preflight, defensive copy, persistence, write failure, memory fallback, corruption, incompatibility, and Reset.
Template immutability and nested mutation-leakage tests across edit, reload, Reset, and Clone.

Demo Preview tests for itinerary add/edit/delete/reorder, time/note changes, Checklist add/edit/toggle/delete, owner/unassigned assignees, Reset confirmation, local-only status, and accessible Desktop/Mobile UI.
Negative tests proving expenses, settlements, tickets, attachments, and Storage remain preview-only and non-editable.

Clone tests proving the current validated Sandbox snapshot is used, allowlist conversion is enforced, retry IDs remain deterministic, prohibited data is excluded, and successful Clone does not reset Sandbox.
Feature Introduction tests for Lobby and Settings entries, FirstRunWelcomeDialog replay, open-Demo/create-trip choices, no onboarding-state changes, and separation from Settings Feature Tour.
App integration tests for load/save/reset callbacks, feature-flag default off, Emulator-only repository, myTrips verification, no Offline Cache, and no production Firebase.
Independent review evidence for Decision compliance, path ownership, template immutability, local-only persistence, Feature Introduction/Tour separation, and zero Rules/dependency/production changes.

QA coverage for reload persistence, Reset isolation, edit flows, Clone edited snapshot, forbidden data, zero cloud writes, Lobby/Settings replay, onboarding eligibility, Desktop Chrome, Mobile Safari, complete Vitest and Playwright regression.
unresolvedQuestions: The original Production Gate questions for Auth, owner/member/invitation authorization, Rules, legacy-room compatibility, rollout, and rollback remain unresolved and are not changed by this Amendment.
Any future request to edit expenses, settlements, tickets, attachments, Storage uploads, or Firebase collaboration in Demo requires a separate Decision amendment.

evidence.claim: The human-confirmed Round 1 requirement defines the editable persistent local Sandbox and replayable Feature Introduction scope.
The independent human cross-review validates immutable-template, untrusted-store, Reset, Clone, onboarding, and preserved-safety requirements.
The original approved Session and six Assignments are immutable audit history and remain execution-disabled.
The Demo source factory is the immutable source-template boundary.
DemoTripPreview is the bounded Sandbox editing and Clone surface.
FirstRunWelcomeDialog provides the high-level feature introduction content to replay without first-run side effects.
AppSettingsMenu already exposes the separate trip-contextual 功能導覽 action.
Offline Trip Cache remains a separate read-only real-trip snapshot boundary.
